name: Calouste Gulbenkian Foundation - Western Armenian Culture Grant ("in view")
deadline: 2025-26 cycle referenced; exact date unconfirmed
requirements: Projects must center Western Armenian language/culture specifically; includes video and animation
strategy: Narrow niche — best fit only for projects explicitly engaging Western Armenian language/culture.
applicationFee: Not specified — many of these are free to apply; verify on official page

name: Eurasiadoc (Docmonde) - Armenian Documentary Scriptwriting Residency
amount: Free program (no cash grant; covers residency costs for selected participants)
deadline: Annual call — check Docmonde/French Embassy in Armenia announcements
requirements: Open to young Armenian author-filmmakers; selects 6/year for a two-week scriptwriting residency with a French trainer
previousWinners: 37 completed Armenian documentaries since 2011, 27 more in production
strategy: Highly selective, oriented toward emerging Armenian documentary voices with strong authorial perspective.
applicationFee: Not specified — many of these are free to apply; verify on official page

name: New Talents Caucasus (CinéDOC-Tbilisi / Noosfera Foundation)
deadline: Annual, tied to CinéDOC-Tbilisi festival cycle
requirements: Open to filmmakers from Georgia, Armenia, Azerbaijan; includes Summer School and Focus Caucasus pitching competition
strategy: Regional Caucasus documentary network-building; more a pitching/training credential than a direct funding source.

name: Creative Europe MEDIA / Creative Europe Desk Armenia
deadline: Multiple calls/year via EU Funding & Tenders Portal
requirements: Eligibility tied to specific Creative Europe MEDIA sub-programmes including Armenia as partner country
strategy: Best approached as a co-production/development funding source rather than a dedicated Armenia-only short film grant — tangential fit.
